title: Tamil Nadu Cabinet expansion updates: 23 more TVK, Congress Ministers take oath; IUML to join Vijay-led government
content: With the latest expansion, a total of 33 Ministers are now part of the TVK-led government in Tamil Nadu, including two from Congress.

title: India sees relations with China in a bilateral context, Beijing has never seen it that way: Vijay Gokhale
content: China’s past decisions to go to war were not driven by only territorial or military objectives, but shaped by wider political and geopolitical factors, according to a new book China’s Wars: The Politics and Diplomacy Behind its Military Coercion by former Ambassador to China Vijay Gokhale

title: CM’s appeal to kids for votes: Plea in Madras High Court seeks an inquiry by Election Commission
content: Litigant also wants an inquiry into alleged distribution of cash for votes by the DMK and AIADMK members during the 2026 Legislative Assembly polls

title: APPTA urges Nara Lokesh to protect SC/ST teachers’ rights in district reorganisation
content: The association warns that preparing seniority lists on merit alone, ignoring the Merit-cum-Roster rule, could push SC/ST teachers down the list and trigger litigation

title: IUML to join TVK-led Cabinet in Tamil Nadu
content: The party’s decision to join the TVK-led government signals the end of its longstanding alliance with the DMK

title: Eight students hospitalised after falling ill amid heatwave in U.P.’s Balrampur
content: The District administration ordered closure of all Kasturba schools in view of the prevailing heat and humidity

title: Calls grow for 110 kV substation in Thamarassery in Kozhikode
content: Proposed substation essential to overcome transmission losses, improve voltage quality, and ensure uninterrupted power supply to nearly 70,000 consumers across the three electrical section limits, Thamarassery, Koduvally, and Omassery, say KSEB officials

title: Ensure that Karnataka complies with SC judgment on Cavuery waters, farmers’ group urges Vijay
content: The Cauvery Rights Retrieval Committee points out that the Cauvery River Protection Committee in Karnataka, headed by Justice (retd.) V. Gopala Gowda, passed a resolution on May 9 urging Karnataka to file a review petition in the Supreme Court

title: Former Speaker Dhanapal announces exit from AIADMK
content: After attending the swearing-in of his son and Rasipuram legislator, D. Logesh Tamilsevan, as Minister in the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK)-led Cabinet, at the Lok Bhavan in Chennai, Mr. Dhanapal told reporters he had not been given “importance” by the AIADMK leadership

title: Victims have long been ‘pushed into a corner’, says Supreme Court
content: The top court was hearing a plea filed by an accused in a chit fund scam seeking the clubbing of FIRs registered across multiple States

title: Three Indians among 274 to Mt. Everest, set new record
content: The three Indian mountaineers who were part of the team that made a World Record are Tulasi Reddi Palpunoori, Sandeep Are and Ajay Pal Singh Dhaliwal

title: Watch: CM Vijay’s expanded cabinet: Who are the new ministers?
content: The members were sworn in by Governor Rajendra Vishwanath Arlekar at the Lok Bhavan, Chennai. Previously, 10 members, including the Chief Minister, took oath on May 10, 2026

title: Telangana hikes minimum wages for workers
content: Revised wages to come into effect from June 1, 2026. A total of 1.11 crore labourers to benefit from revised pay structure: Telangana CM Revanth Reddy

title: Kadambrayar pollution: Kerala State Pollution Control Board starts inspecting erring units to fix environmental compensation
content: Board officials say that a final count of violators will become clear only after studying the report submitted by local bodies sharing the banks of the river. Violators include industrial and commercial units, apartment and residential complexes, and hotels

title: India-Africa Forum Summit postponed in wake of Ebola virus outbreak
content: The cancellation of the Fourth India-Africa Forum Summit is being viewed as a setback to the India-Africa relation though officials are hinting that the decision was taken in the best interest of all stakeholders

title: Police arrested Bageerath, he did not surrender: Telangana CM
content: Telangana had emerged as a model for the country by conducting caste census along with general census: Revanth Reddy

title: Rajnath Singh inaugurates Indian War Memorial in South Korea, remembers war veterans
content: Rajnath Singh was joined by the Republic of Korea’s Minister of Patriots & Veterans Affairs in unveiling the war memorial at the Imjingak Park in Seoul

title: Bengaluru’s shelved stray dog microchipping plan back on track
content: Union Ministry of Animal Husbandry directs municipal corporations to implant radio frequency identification (RFID) tags during animal birth control (ABC) surgery

title: Parole for Periye twin murder convicts: Home Minister Ramesh Chennithala seeks report
content: Chennithala directs Additional Chief Secretary (Home) to look into the issue and submit a report. All aspects surrounding the decision to allow parole should be examined, according to the Minister’s direction

title: Heatwave alert in Nandyal, Kurnool till May 26; Collectors order water, ORS at public places
content: People urged to avoid the afternoon sun and do outdoor work before 9 a.m. or after 5 p.m.; officials told to set up water kiosks, ORS supplies and pots for birds and strays

title: Visakhapatnam air pollution: Citizens’ Forum seeks six-point action plan from administration
content: PM10 levels have risen 32.9% since 2017-18; the forum wants curbs on coal and construction dust, tighter vehicle checks and a public pollution dashboard

title: Cockroach Janata Party’s X handle withheld, founder starts new account
content: The movement became an internet sensation, garnering over 14.5 million followers on Instagram, surpassing BJP’s 8.8 million follower count

title: In unexpected twist, both judges on SC Bench withdraw from delivering verdict in 2011 Duraisamy-Stalin election dispute
content: The withdrawal of the two judges was quite unexpected as the appeal was extensively heard by the Bench across several days in January and February, before being reserved on February 19 for judgment

title: Ex-Kerala Finance Minister Thomas Isaac questions UDF’s ‘empty treasury’ claim
content: Isaac says UDF White Paper should clarify matters on revenue arrears, GST revenue from gold sales, and Integrated Goods and Services Tax settlement

title: ‘Drishyam 3’ movie review: Mohanlal is the saving grace in the least effective film of the franchise
content: Mohanlal’s interpretation of Georgekutty’s character saves the film from getting completely bogged down by director Jeethu Joseph’s outdated visual style and heavy-handed drama

title: From ‘virus’ to ‘tsunami’: How outgoing Chief Ministers, leaders of Tamil Nadu viewed election results since 1967
content: In a recent comment on the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam winning the 2026 Tamil Nadu elections, DMK president M.K. Stalin described the victory as a “new and attractive tsunami of illusion”

title: U.P. Governor mandates uniform for students in all State colleges, universities
content: Governor stressed the need for the holistic development of students and directed teachers to attend classes on time and ensure the regular attendance of students

title: U.S. team may visit India next month for trade talks: Piyush Goyal
content: He said India needs to work closely with the U.S. in areas such as technology, innovation, high-precision defence, digital data centres, quantum computing equipment, and medical devices.

title: Over 74% turnout in Falta repoll till 3 p.m.; voting peaceful
content: A total of 2.36 lakh electors -- 1,21,300 men, 1,15,135 women and nine third gender individuals -- are eligible to exercise their franchise in the repoll, which started at 7 am and will continue till 6 pm.

title: CM Vijay-led Cabinet in Tamil Nadu expands to 33; Congress joins government after 59 years
content: Mr. Vijay’s Cabinet is likely to undergo one more round of expansion, as the TVK has extended an open invitation to the VCK and IUML, both of which contested as part of the DMK-led Secular Progressive Alliance (SPA) and have two MLAs each

title: West Bengal government issues orders prohibiting officials to speak to media
content: Trinamool Congress general secretary Abhishek Banerjee shared the circular on social media a and said, that the phrase ‘complete prohibition’ echoing through this circular like a warning ”not to protect governance, but to enforce silence upon Govt employees across Bengal”

title: India abstains on UNGA resolution on ICJ climate opinion
content: The resolution was adopted in the 193-member General Assembly on May 20, with 141 votes in favour, eight against and 28 abstentions, including by India

title: Kerala govt’s decision to set up dedicated Senior Citizens’ Welfare department hailed as timely
content: In Kerala, ageing and social transformation challenges, such as issues of isolation and poor mental health of the elderly, are also overshadowed by the staggering burden of chronic multimorbidities among the aged

title: Eviction drive at Malayidomthuruth in Kerala: Police register case against over 50 persons
content: Case registered based on a complaint filed by senior lawyer Jayapalan M.K, who led Advocate Commission that tried to carry out the eviction drive

title: Synthetic variants, vanishing trees threaten legacy of the Nilgiris eucalyptus oil
content: An application seeking a Geographical Indication (GI) tag for the Nilgiris Eucalyptus Thailam has been filed, but some manufacturers express doubt over whether the move can revive the industry

title: Will BJP leaders take public transport daily, or just for photoshoot: D.K. Shivakumar
content: Karnataka Deputy CM D.K. Shivakumar accuses BJP leaders of using public transport only for photoshoots

title: Kerala Cabinet 2026: Full list of Ministers
content: The detailed portfolios will be submitted to the Governor and will be published via gazette notification

title: Marie Wilson is T.N.’s new Finance Minister, Sengottaiyan gets Revenue; portfolios allocated to new ministers of Vijay-led Cabinet
content: Mr. Vijay retained his existing departments and was additionally allotted Special Initiatives, Poverty Alleviation and Rural Indebtedness

title: Fake call centres duping US citizens busted in Bengaluru, man from Delhi, another from UP arrested
content: Police said the call centre employees used fake identities resembling those of U.S. officials to gain the trust of victims

title: Hyderabad airport posts Ebola advisory for passengers from three countries
content: Health authorities have asked travellers developing symptoms within 21 days of arrival to immediately seek medical care and cooperate with public health screening measures

title: Suo motu case against top Karnataka officials after Lokayukta raids expose illegal sand mining in Bagalkot
content: Director of Mines and Geology Department in Bengaluru, Deputy Director of Mines and Geology in Bagalkot, the Bagalkot Deputy Commissioner, and tahsildars of Badami, Mudhol, Jamakhandi, Hunagund and Ilkal taluks are among those named in case

title: Milma to hike milk price by ₹4/litre from June 1
content: Customers will have to pay ₹28 for a 500-ml packet of homogenised toned milk, which is priced at ₹26 now. The prices of other variants of packaged liquid milk  and other milk products sold by Milma will also go up correspondingly

title: Umar Khalid is in jail to ensure society doesn't turn into prison: Prakash Raj at Bengaluru event
content: The event focused on Mr. Khalid’s writings, incarceration, and the broader political and social context reflected in the book

title: BJP opens two-day Deendayal Upadhyaya training programme for State leaders
content: The party plans welfare outreach programmes from June 5 to 21; a State leader praises Modi’s global standing and faults the YSRCP on fuel taxes

title: Semi-naked dacoits rob string of houses Uttar Pradesh's Budaun
content: In the early hours of May 20, armed intruders scaled the wall of farmer Nizakat's house and held his family hostage

title: Twisha Sharma death case: Absconding husband applies for bail in Jabalpur High Court
content: A magistrate court had earlier rejected his anticipatory bail application while granting it to his mother, Giribala Singh, a retired District Sessions Judge.

title: Malayali youngsters develop rail hygiene kit addressing sanitary concerns over long distance train travel
content: Rail hygiene kit by Travel Essentia, a startup founded by four Malayali youngsters, consists of 10 components for maintaining personal hygiene

title: Government issues Ebola advisory for passengers arriving from DR Congo, Uganda and South Sudan
content: Union Health Ministry officials clarified that no case of Ebola has been detected in India so far; it advised passengers to watch out for symptoms such as fever, weakness, headache, muscle pain, vomiting, diarrhoea, sore throat and unexplained bleeding

title: TVK-led Tamil Nadu government: full list of Ministers
content: The specific portfolios for the nine Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) ministers were previously announced on Saturday (May 16, 2026), with Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay retaining key departments

title: Newly elected MLAs sworn in as 16th Kerala Assembly convenes
content: Pro Tem Speaker G. Sudhakaran administers oath to MLAs-designate over a three-and-a-half-hour-long ceremony

title: ‘Enriched uranium must remain in Iran’: Khamenei rejects key US demand; peace talks falter
content: Iran's Supreme Leader has ordered the country's near-weapons-grade enriched uranium stockpile to remain within Iran, complicating peace talks. This directive hardens Tehran's stance, as the US and Israel demand its removal. The move comes amid ongoing regional conflict and deep suspicion of further US airstrikes.

title: Air India flight suffers tailstrike while landing at Bengaluru airport
content: An Air India flight experienced a tailstrike upon landing at Bengaluru airport today. While the aircraft touched down safely with all passengers and crew unharmed, such incidents necessitate thorough inspections for structural integrity. The airline confirmed the safe arrival of the plane, with further details pending.

title: Govt’s proposed anti-doping amendment could send doctors, coaches to jail - Explained
content: The proposed amendments have been uploaded on the sports ministry’s website for public feedback, with June 18 set as the deadline for suggestions.

title: Oath drama in TN: Governor fumes as Congress MLA raises slogans hailing Rajiv Gandhi, Rahul
content: During a Tamil Nadu Cabinet expansion, Governor Arlekar cautioned Congress MLA S. Rajesh Kumar to adhere strictly to the written oath after he deviated to praise party leaders. Kumar, along with other new ministers, took their oaths at Lok Bhavan. Subsequently, other TVK legislators also added party-specific slogans to their affirmations.

title: Google co-founder Sergey Brin donates $500,000 to group fighting ‘overpaid CEO tax’
content: Google co-founder Sergey Brin is injecting significant funds into San Francisco's local politics, backing a business-friendly tax proposal while opposing a controversial "Overpaid CEO Tax."  This move, following his relocation from Silicon Valley, reflects Brin's broader campaign against wealth redistribution measures, which he links to his personal experience fleeing socialism.

title: 'Tension nahi lene ka': Vaibhav Sooryavanshi' message to Sangakkara before 10 sixes carnage
content: Rajasthan Royals' 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi delivered a match-winning 93 off 38 balls, including 10 sixes, to chase down 221 against LSG.  He reassured coach Sangakkara, stating, 'ho jayega,' and then proceeded to keep playoff hopes alive. Sooryavanshi also became the youngest Indian to hit 50 IPL sixes in a season.

title: RG Kar rape-murder case: HC asks CBI to probe 'evidence tampering' charge
content: The Calcutta High Court has ordered the CBI to form a Special Investigation Team to probe allegations of suppression in the R G Kar Medical College rape and murder case. The SIT, led by the CBI's joint director (East), will investigate claims that aspects of the case were "hushed up." A detailed status report is due by June 25.

title: Virat Kohli Test comeback loading? Fans erupt after childhood coach’s cryptic remark
content: Speculation about Virat Kohli's potential return to Test cricket has resurfaced following comments from his childhood coach, Rajkumar Sharma. Sharma hinted that discussions regarding a comeback are underway, igniting excitement among fans who believe the former captain still has unfinished business in the longest format. Kohli retired from Test cricket in May 2025 after a distinguished 14-year career.

title: Mark Zuckerberg's email to employees has a 'goodbye note' for those laid off
content: Meta has initiated significant layoffs, sending emails to thousands of employees whose positions are being eliminated.  Company leadership confirmed the decision to reduce headcount for increased efficiency.  Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg also sent a company-wide memo expressing gratitude to departing employees and outlining future company direction.  He assured remaining staff that no further company-wide layoffs are expected this year.

title: CEO of America's largest bank: To reduce our jobs down the road, we will hire fewer bankers
content: JP Morgan CEO Jamie Dimon predicts artificial intelligence will reduce some banking jobs.  The bank plans to hire more AI specialists and fewer traditional bankers.  This shift will be managed through employee turnover and retraining.  Other global banks also see AI impacting support roles and back-office operations.  The pace of change requires careful societal consideration.

title: Rupee under pressure: How RBI may try to stabilise the currency
content: The Indian rupee has fallen nearly 6% against the US dollar due to Middle East tensions, prompting the Reserve Bank of India to consider measures like interest rate hikes and attracting dollar inflows. Officials are exploring a foreign currency deposit scheme for NRIs and a potential sovereign dollar bond to stabilize the currency.

title: Iran war lessons: US eyes low-cost cruise missiles; what are India’s plans?
content: Global conflicts highlight the need for affordable, long-range weapons. The US is procuring thousands of new, mass-producible cruise missiles, the Barracuda-500M, for over $2.6 million each. India, too, is seeking similar low-cost, high-volume strike capabilities, including drones and missiles, under its upcoming Defence Acquisition Procedure 2026.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' box office collection Day 64: Can Ranveer Singh film net Rs. 1150 crore?
content: With the JioHotstar premiere locked in for June 4, 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' is entering the final stretch of its historic theatrical run with India net at Rs. 1,146.35 crore, Rs. 1,150 crore virtually certain, and the 'Pushpa 2' domestic record sitting Rs. 87.75 crore away. With its highly anticipated dual-platform OTT release officially locked for June 4, 2026, the film is racing against time to make the most out its final theatrical run. Amid this, fans looking forward to revisiting the high-octane spy drama can catch it on streaming platforms next month. 'Dhurandhar 2' is opting for a unique dual-platform digital release of JioHotstar premiere on June 4. The movie is already streaming on Netflix under the title 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge — Raw and Uncut', boasting extended graphic sequences.

title: Ronda Rousey’s net worth 2026: Earnings, endorsements and lifestyle
content: Ronda Rousey, a trailblazer in combat sports, is projected to have a net worth of nearly $14 million by 2026. Her fortune stems from lucrative UFC and WWE careers, acting, endorsements, and book sales. Rousey's impact on women's MMA and her transition to WWE cemented her status as a crossover star, ensuring her continued relevance and earning potential.

title: Bryson DeChambeau slammed after moon landing claims, PGA flop
content: Bryson DeChambeau went viral after questioning the Apollo 11 moon landing footage during a podcast appearance. The comments came after he missed the cut at the PGA Championship. Fans heavily criticized his opinion online, while DeChambeau also opened up about possibly choosing YouTube content creation over professional golf in the future.

title: Jeff Bezos favored to buy Seahawks from Microsoft co-founder’s estate
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos is projected as the leading buyer for the Seattle Seahawks. The NFL team was owned by late former Microsoft co-founder Paul Allen. After his passing in 2018, it is now managed by her sister Jody Allen under the Paul G. Allen Trust. It is now officially up for sale. Jeff Bezos has shown interest in owning a local NFL franchise for over seven years. The sale process is expected to conclude by the 2026 offseason.

title: Bhojshala verdict: Fresh plea seeks removal of 'Islamic symbols', excavation
content: Following the Madhya Pradesh High Court's verdict recognizing Dhar's Bhojshala complex as Goddess Vagdevi's temple, Hindu petitioners have filed new pleas. They are demanding free entry for devotees, further excavation, and the opening of a locked room, asserting the court's order necessitates unrestricted access and removal of 'unauthorised Islamic symbols'.

title: JEE Advanced 2026 response sheet releasing today: Steps to download, answer key release details
content: IIT Roorkee will release the JEE Advanced 2026 response sheets today, May 21, at 5 pm on jeeadv.ac.in. Candidates can check their marked answers and compare them with the provisional answer key, which will be released on May 25. The final answer key and results are scheduled for June 1.

title: 'Time will answer': DK Shivakumar's cryptic reply amid Karnataka CM buzz
content: Karnataka Deputy Chief Minister DK Shivakumar stated that "time will decide" his potential elevation to Chief Minister, amidst growing speculation of a leadership change in the state's Congress government. This comes as the Siddaramaiah-led government approaches a significant milestone, fueling discussions about a possible power-sharing arrangement.

title: Plea filed in high court over 30 years delay in PESA Rules for tribal areas in Odisha
content: Tribal organizations are taking a stand by petitioning the Orissa High Court, claiming the state's chronic delays in enforcing the PESA Act, 1996. Despite being enacted almost three decades ago, Odisha has not put into place the necessary legislation, resulting in tribal Gram Sabhas lacking the constitutional authority needed for self-rule and the safeguarding of their resources.

title: 'When something is important enough, you do it even if the ... ': Quote of the day by Musk
content: Elon Musk's philosophy, "When something is important enough, you do it even if the odds are not in your favour," emphasizes pursuing passions despite challenges. This quote teaches children the importance of identifying what truly matters, overcoming fear, and persisting through failures to achieve extraordinary goals.

title: 'Don’t do BJP politics': DMK fumes as Vande Mataram played before state anthem at Vijay govt oath event
content: A controversy erupted in Tamil Nadu over the recitation order of national and state songs during a swearing-in ceremony. The DMK criticized the Vijay government for prioritizing 'Vande Mataram' over the Tamil invocation song, accusing them of disrespecting Tamil culture and adopting BJP's politics.

title: 'Why can't I question past conduct': Twisha mother-in-law's counter on viral audio clip
content: A leaked audio recording allegedly features retired judge Giri Bala Singh, mother-in-law of deceased Twisha Sharma, engaging in character shaming and discussing Twisha's past relationships. Her son-in-law, Major Harshit Sharma, confronts her about the alleged interrogation of his sister. Meanwhile, police are intensifying efforts to apprehend Twisha's absconding husband, Samarth Singh, in a dowry harassment case.

title: 'Drishyam 3' BO collection day 1: Mohanlal starrer aims for 50cr opening
content: Mohanlal's 'Drishyam 3' is off to a roaring start, with early estimates suggesting a Rs 6.25 Cr net collection on its opening day in India. Buoyed by massive advance bookings, the film is poised for a Rs 50+ crore worldwide opening. Audiences are praising the gripping narrative and emotional depth, anticipating another blockbuster for the franchise.

title: 'Line between defamation & criticism is thin': Delhi HC on Raghav Chadha's personality rights plea
content: Delhi High Court has reserved its decision on a plea by MP Raghav Chadha. He sought protection against AI-generated deepfakes and manipulated content. The court noted that political leaders often face criticism and satire. Arguments focused on the line between defamation and fair political commentary. The case highlights challenges in balancing free speech with dignity in the digital age.

title: 'We could have handled it better': Tamim Iqbal on Bangladesh’s T20 WC pull out
content: Former Bangladesh captain Tamim Iqbal opens up on his transition into cricket administration after a heart attack, Bangladesh’s fast bowling rise, the future of India-Bangladesh relations, the growing influence of T20 leagues. Bangladesh Cricket Board's interim president also shares his vision to restore Bangladesh cricket’s reputation, which has taken a beating after they decided to pull out from the T20 World Cup.

title: Rupee, CAD under watch, US trade talks on track: Goyal says India will 'come out winner'
content: Union Minister Piyush Goyal addressed concerns over the rupee's decline and widening current account deficit, stating the government is considering measures to manage the situation. He also highlighted significant US investment commitments and confirmed ongoing trade deal discussions are proceeding as scheduled.

title: Karnataka second PUC exam 2 result released: Direct link to download scorecards here
content: Karnataka's second PUC Exam 2 results are out today, May 21st, at 1 pm. Students can check their scorecards on karresults.nic.in and the KSEAB PUC result portal by entering their registration number and stream. Results are also accessible via the Karnataka One app. Verify all details on your scorecard carefully.

title: Rs 600 crore rail land, Eid fears: Why Mumbai eviction drive turned chaotic
content: Western Railway commenced a massive anti-encroachment drive at Garib Nagar, Mumbai, demolishing hundreds of structures near Bandra station to reclaim valuable railway land. The operation, aimed at facilitating railway expansion, faced resistance and violence, resulting in injuries and detentions. Residents allege inadequate rehabilitation and express distress over demolitions preceding Eid.

title: Muga silk stole for Meloni, mangoes for UAE Prez: What PM Modi gifted leaders during 5-nation tour
content: Prime Minister Modi's recent five-nation tour saw him present world leaders with culturally rich Indian gifts. From Assam's golden Muga silk to Manipur's Shirui Lily stoles and Agra's marble inlay boxes, these presents showcased India's diverse traditional crafts, agricultural bounty, and musical heritage, fostering cultural exchange.

title: 'Amra Korbo Joy': Bangladesh celebrate Pakistan Test sweep with special song - Watch
content: Bangladesh achieved a historic 2-0 Test series whitewash against Pakistan, culminating in an emotional dressing room celebration. Players united to sing "Amra Korbo Joy" after their dominant 78-run victory in the second Test. This triumph signifies Bangladesh's growing confidence and consistency in Test cricket.

title: 42 aircraft, over $29 billion: What Trump’s Iran operation cost the US military
content: Operation Epic Fury, initiated under the leadership of Donald Trump, saw the U.S. military suffer grave losses, with 42 aircraft—comprising fighter jets and drones—sustaining damage or being lost entirely. This aggressive strike prompted swift and vigorous reprisal from Iran, bringing the region to a boiling point.

title: Gold, silver outlook: Will gold head to Rs 1.70 lakh/10 grams & silver hit Rs 3 lakh/kg?
content: Gold and silver price prediction today: While MCX Silver is consolidating on the weekly chart, gold is eyeing its next meaningful target of Rs 1.7 lakh, says Abhilash Koikkara, Head - Forex & Commodities, Nuvama Professional Clients Group.

title: NEET UG 2026 exam fee refund process explained: Key NTA rules for students
content: The NTA is set to roll out a refund portal this Thursday for students retaking NEET UG 2026, providing refunds that vary by category, though gateway fees are excluded. Re-examinations will occur on June 21 without any added costs.

title: ‘Neck pain, dizziness ...’: Watson reveals worrying injury update on Raghuvanshi
content: Kolkata Knight Riders secured a crucial win against Mumbai Indians, but their victory was overshadowed by a worrying injury to star batter Angkrish Raghuvanshi. Colliding with a teammate while attempting a catch, Raghuvanshi suffered neck pain, dizziness, and headaches, forcing him out of the match. The franchise is concerned about the impact on their playoff push.

title: UPSC calendar 2027: Civil Services prelims on May 23; check complete schedule here
content: The Union Public Service Commission has unveiled its 2027 exam calendar, featuring key dates for major recruitment drives. The Civil Services Preliminary Examination is scheduled for May 23, 2027, with the Mains commencing on August 20, 2027. Aspirants can find notification release dates, application deadlines, and examination durations for numerous other crucial tests on the official UPSC website.

title: 'Promiscuity can be a habit': New audio captures exchange between Twisha’s brother, mother-in-law
content: A leaked audio recording allegedly features retired judge Giri Bala Singh questioning Twisha Sharma about her past sexual history and fidelity.  Meanwhile, police have increased the reward for information leading to the arrest of Twisha's husband, Samarth Singh, who is wanted in a dowry harassment case.

title: Twisha Sharma's Mother-In-Law Giribala Singh Gets 3rd Notice For Questioning
content: The legal team of Twisha's family has raised a series of questions over the handling of her body and the post-mortem process.

title: Murder, Parole, Then Bollywood Career: Gujarat Actor Arrested After 12 Years
content: According to police, Hemant Vaishnav was convicted in connection with a murder case linked to a 2005 clash in Ahmedabad's Naroda area.

title: Unidentified Gunmen Strike Again, Wanted Al-Badr Commander Shot Dead In PoK
content: Arjamand Gulzar alias Burhan Hamza, who was killed by unidentified gunmen, was wanted in India for spearheading an elaborate terror network in Jammu and Kashmir.

title: Delhi Woman Dies By Suicide Over Dowry Harassment, Husband Among 2 Arrested
content: In a statement, Deputy Commissioner of Police (West) Darade Sharad Bhaskar identified the deceased as "wife of Raju Singh".

title: Mojtaba Hardens Stand On Key US Demand, Says Uranium Must Stay In Iran: Report
content: Iran's top officials believe that sending the material abroad would leave the country more vulnerable to future attacks by the United States and Israel.

title: 16 Arrested For Stone Throwing During Anti-Encroachment Drive In Mumbai
content: The Western Railway authorities began clearing unauthorised structures starting on Tuesday as part of an infrastructure and land redevelopment exercise.

title: Twisha Sharma Death And The Public Declarations Of Her Mother-In-Law
content: Twisha Sharma's family alleges harassment by her husband, Samarth Singh, and his family, while retired judge Giribala Singh has been making disparaging remarks about her daughter-in-law.

title: "Mit Jaoge": Pakistani Senator's Unprovoked Anti-India Speech Sparks Outrage
content: US-based political commentator and journalist Amy Mek shared a long thread about the event titled "Maarka-e-Haq", on X, describing the address as "a direct threat from Pakistan on US soil."

title: Crores Lost In Legal Fights, Yet Why Aren't Indian Families Writing Wills?
content: About Rs 40-60 lakh crore will transfer between generations in the next decade. Without a will, a significant portion may get lost as legal cost.

title: Prashant Kishor May Have A New Task In Maharashtra. It's Under Wraps Now
content: After the Bihar polls debacle, Prashant Kishor, say reports, is in touch with the Sunetra Pawar-led Nationalist Congress Party, or NCP, to help revamp the regional party.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Twisha Sharma And The Case Of The Ever-Innocent Indian Husband
content: Within 72 hours of Twisha's death, a media narrative had overtaken the forensic one: "erratic behaviour", "history of depression", and alleged drug use. None of it was ever substantiated in a chargesheet. Because that was never the point.

title: AI Engineer Says Google Fired Him After Protest Over Israel Deals: Report
content: The engineer distributed flyers inside Google DeepMind’s London office where he accused the company of providing “military AI” to forces “committing genocide.”

title: Rohit Shetty's Home Shooters Fled 1st Time, Then A Call From Lawrence Bishnoi's Brother
content: Five rounds were fired at Bollywood film director and producer Rohit Shetty's house in Mumbai on February 1.

title: Man Stabs Wife In Sleep, Then Dies By Suicide In UP's Sitapur
content: The couple had been married for a year, and family members stated that their relationship was strained with frequent quarrels.

content: your active subscription(s) Account subscription benefits alongside Premium Stories, Editorials, Opinions and more . title: Meet Mbah Sadiman: Man who sold his goats to transform barren hills into forest
content: villagers in a drought-prone region of central Java believed Mbah Sadiman was wasting his life . the elderly farmer spent decades planting banyan trees across barren slopes . he even sold his goats and used much of his own savings, despite being mocked by neighbours who considered the effort pointless .
